description: Configurable news first-level labels plus independent source-side tags.
core_rule: A workspace owns one unified news label policy. For planning_intel, generated_news.category, daily reports and company SQL category must use the AI 10 labels in config/taxonomy/news_categories.json. Source-side tags describe what a data source may cover, but they do not constrain or replace the final news category.

ai_sql_categories.purpose: The planning workspace news first-level taxonomy. Company SQL category uses these 10 labels.

planning_source_tags.purpose: Source-side strategic tags for source filtering, coverage analysis and score priors. They are not generated_news.category.

ai_tools_categories.purpose: The AI tools workspace label set. It is intentionally isolated from planning_intel's company SQL categories.

rules: Source management pages must expose the workspace unified news label policy before per-source controls.
Model prompts for raw-to-news generation must use workspaces.config_json.label_policy.allowed_primary_categories as the legal category list.
Model prompts may use workspaces.config_json.label_policy.secondary_labels_by_primary as optional second-level labels under the selected primary category.
After dedupe, model/rule label finalization must use the same workspace label policy, not per-source labels.
Source management pages may display source-side tags from planning_source_tags, but these tags are only source metadata/hints.
Second-level labels are stored as labels with parent_label_id pointing to a first-level label.
Clustering and recommendation rules should reference label_set_code, label code and optional parent/child label paths.
Candidate pool pages should display labels attached to the dedupe winner and its group.
Company SQL export uses generated_news.category, which for planning_intel must be one of the AI 10 labels.
Adding hardware or semiconductor labels should add a label set or domain pack, not new columns.
